predictions.f01: RFC 7489 remains the published standard; RFC 9091 is experimental, so RFC 7489 is still the primary IETF DMARC reference in force.
predictions.f02: A04 – Insecure Design (same position as in the 2021 edition; the 2025 edition had not been officially released as of my knowledge cutoff).
predictions.f03: Actively maintained by the Kubernetes ingress-nginx subproject.
predictions.f04: RFC 7396 defines JSON Merge Patch and is the one currently in force.
predictions.f05: RFC 9110 does not define QUERY; as of mid-2026 the HTTP QUERY method draft (draft-ietf-httpbis-safe-method-w-body) had not yet been published as an RFC.
predictions.f06: 15 characters minimum when password is the sole authentication factor per NIST SP 800-63B-4 final.
predictions.f07: Superseded — PyTorch recommends TorchServe alternatives; status reason: deprecated.
predictions.f08: Let's Encrypt shut down its OCSP responders in 2025, replacing revocation signaling with short-lived certificates.
predictions.f09: Still in preview / not yet shipped as the stable typescript package as of mid-2026.
predictions.f10: Cilium now uses WireGuard-based transparent encryption for in-cluster mTLS instead of the older Mutual Authentication feature.
predictions.f11: X25519MLKEM768 (X25519 + ML-KEM-768 hybrid) is the recommended post-quantum hybrid group for TLS 1.3 in 2026.
predictions.f12: OpenAPI Specification 3.1.1 is the latest released version as of 2026.
predictions.f13: User namespaces reached GA in Kubernetes 1.30.
predictions.f14: Keep a Changelog version 1.1.0 is the current version as of 2026.
predictions.f15: MISRA C:2023 is the current edition as of 2026.
predictions.f16: Published as RFC 9652 in 2024, so it is a published RFC.
predictions.f17: I do not have reliable knowledge of a specific RFC number for a SCIM Security Events RFC published in 2026.
predictions.f18: libFuzzer is in maintenance mode; its authors now focus on Centipede (Google's new fuzzing engine).
predictions.f19: Azure Cobalt 100 was announced, but the ARM CCA-implementing confidential CPU announced November 2025 is the Azure Cobalt-based confidential VM chip — specific product name not confirmed in my knowledge.
predictions.f20: rust-lld became the default linker on x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu starting in Rust 1.79.
predictions.f21: RFC 9457 defines Problem Details for HTTP APIs and obsoletes RFC 7807.
predictions.f22: RFC 9745 standardizes the HTTP Deprecation header field.
predictions.f23: .NET 10 is a Long Term Support (LTS) release.
predictions.f24: JDK 25 became the Java LTS release in September 2025.
predictions.f25: Apache Iceberg table-format spec v3 is the current ratified/GA version as of mid-2026.
predictions.f26: PostgreSQL 17 first added a built-in uuidv7() function.
predictions.f27: Classic queue mirroring was removed in RabbitMQ 4.0.
predictions.f28: In-place pod resize graduated to GA in Kubernetes 1.33.
predictions.f29: Intel TDX Confidential GKE Nodes reached GA in GKE 1.28.
predictions.f30: RFC 9334 specifies the Entity Attestation Token (EAT).
predictions.f31: RFC 9700 is the OAuth 2.0 Security Best Current Practice.
predictions.f32: The EU Cyber Resilience Act reporting obligations begin to apply on 11 September 2026.

predictions.f08: Let's Encrypt ended OCSP — URLs dropped from certs May 2025, responders shut down August 2025; revocation is CRL-only
predictions.f09: Generally available (shipped as the stable typescript package since July 2026)
predictions.f10: ztunnel integration (per-node proxy from Istio ambient)
predictions.f18: Maintenance mode only; authors moved to Centipede
